data.q: What is gRPC primarily used for in PHP API integration?
data.e: gRPC is a high-performance RPC framework used to enable communication between client and server applications.

data.q: Which protocol does gRPC use as its transport layer?
data.e: gRPC uses HTTP/2 for transport to enable multiplexing, flow control, and efficient binary framing.

data.q: What is the role of Protocol Buffers (protobuf) in gRPC PHP integration?
data.e: Protocol Buffers define message formats and services that are serialized efficiently over the network.

data.q: How do you generate PHP classes from .proto files?
data.e: The protoc compiler with the PHP plugin generates PHP source files from .proto definitions.

data.q: Which PHP package is essential to install to use gRPC client and server interfaces?
data.e: grpc/grpc is the official PHP extension and library for gRPC support.

data.q: What command is commonly used to add the gRPC PHP package via Composer?
data.e: The correct command to add the gRPC package is `composer require grpc/grpc`.

data.q: In a gRPC PHP service, what class is generated that extends the base service implementation?
o: ServiceXXXClient (where XXX is the service name)
data.e: The protoc compiler generates a client class named ServiceXXXClient for the respective service.

data.q: What method signature is used in PHP to call a unary gRPC method asynchronously?
data.e: Unary calls use the method name directly, optionally with metadata and options arguments.

data.q: Which of the following is a correct step to implement a gRPC server in PHP?
o: Create a PHP class that implements the generated service interface
Use htaccess to redirect calls to server.php
data.e: You define a class implementing the generated abstract service class methods to handle RPCs.

data.q: Which data format does Protocol Buffers use for message serialization?
data.e: Protocol Buffers serialize data in an efficient, compact binary format.

data.q: How are streaming RPCs supported in gRPC PHP?
o: Only server streaming is supported, client streaming is not
Both client and server streaming are supported using stream observers
Streaming is not supported in PHP implementation
Only unary is supported in PHP
data.e: gRPC supports unary, client streaming, server streaming, and bidirectional streaming RPCs in PHP.

data.q: Which extension must be enabled in PHP to support gRPC native functionality?
data.e: The grpc PHP extension (`grpc.so`) must be installed and enabled to run gRPC code.

data.q: What is the command used to compile .proto files with gRPC PHP plugin?
data.e: You must specify the grpc plugin with --grpc_out and plugin option to generate gRPC service classes.

data.q: What will happen if you omit the service definitions in your .proto file when using gRPC?
o: Only message classes are generated, no RPC methods
Full client and server code are generated
data.e: Without service definitions, protoc generates only message classes and no service stubs.

data.q: Which PHP function is used to start the gRPC server?
data.e: The start() method on a gRPC server object begins listening for incoming requests.
